source.note: Almonds — water_per_kg: 13,080 L/kg; averaged with ScienceDirect California study (source 7, 10,240 L/kg) → final 11,660 L/kg.

source.title: Mekonnen & Hoekstra (2010) — The Green, Blue and Grey Water Footprint of Crops and Crop Products (UNESCO-IHE Report 47)
ScienceDirect — Almond Water Footprint Study
source.note: Almonds — green_water_per_kg: 2,321 L/kg for California orchards (Marston et al. 2018, Ecological Indicators; derived from the study's blue+green component breakdown for the California production region average).

source.title: Mekonnen & Hoekstra (2010) — The Green, Blue and Grey Water Footprint of Crops and Crop Products (UNESCO-IHE Report 47)
ScienceDirect — Almond Water Footprint Study
source.note: Almonds — blue_water_per_kg: 4,026 L/kg for California orchards (Marston et al. 2018). California almonds are predominantly irrigated; blue water represents ~39% of total footprint vs. ~23% green and ~38% grey. This is lower than the Mekonnen & Hoekstra global average (source 67: 6,349 L/kg blue) because M&H's global production-weighted figure includes Mediterranean and Middle Eastern rain-fed almond production which elevates the total differently, and M&H report a higher overall total of 13,080 L/kg.

source.note: Brazil nuts — emissions_per_kg: ~0.1–0.3 kg CO2e/kg (P&N 2018 range for wild-harvested forest products; brazil nuts qualify as a zero-input forest harvest with no cultivation, irrigation, fertilizer, or tillage; the 0.2 value is consistent with the lower bound of the P&N dataset for tree-based foods requiring no land conversion or cultivation inputs).

source.note: Brazil nuts — emissions_per_kg: ~0.2 kg CO2e/kg (P&N 2018 via OWID GHG chart; wild-harvested from Amazon rainforest canopy trees with zero cultivation inputs; GHG footprint consists almost entirely of harvest transport; the lowest value of any nut in the P&N dataset).

source.note: Peanuts — yield_kg_ha: Industry yield data.

source.title: Mekonnen & Hoekstra — Water Footprint of Crops (Report 47)
source.note: Peanuts — water_per_kg: 3,740 L/kg; averaged with secondary sources → final 3,537 L/kg. Report 47 gives 2,782 L/kg for in-shell peanuts; 3,740 reflects shelled/edible kernel basis after accounting for ~74% kernel recovery. Difference is within expected range for basis discrepancy.

source.title: Mekonnen & Hoekstra (2010) — The Green, Blue and Grey Water Footprint of Crops and Crop Products (UNESCO-IHE Report 47)

source.note: Peanuts — water_per_kg: 3,740 L/kg; averaged with secondary sources → final 3,537 L/kg. Report 47 gives 2,782 L/kg for in-shell peanuts; 3,740 reflects shelled/edible kernel basis after accounting for ~74% kernel recovery. Difference is within expected range for basis discrepancy.

source.title: Mekonnen & Hoekstra — Water Footprint of Crops (Report 47)
source.note: Peanuts — water_per_kg: 3,740 L/kg; averaged with secondary sources → final 3,537 L/kg. Report 47 gives 2,782 L/kg for in-shell peanuts; 3,740 reflects shelled/edible kernel basis after accounting for ~74% kernel recovery. Difference is within expected range for basis discrepancy.

source.title: Mekonnen & Hoekstra — Water Footprint of Crops (Report 47)
source.note: Peanuts — water_per_kg: 3,740 L/kg; averaged with secondary sources → final 3,537 L/kg. Report 47 gives 2,782 L/kg for in-shell peanuts; 3,740 reflects shelled/edible kernel basis after accounting for ~74% kernel recovery. Difference is within expected range for basis discrepancy.

source.title: Poore & Nemecek (2018) — Reducing food's environmental impacts through producers and consumers (Science)
source.note: Peanuts — emissions_per_kg: ~3.2 kg CO2e/kg (P&N 2018 groundnuts; distinct from tree nuts as a legume — nitrogen fixation offsets some fertilizer N2O but tillage, drying, and aflatoxin management add GHG; global production in India, China, US, Nigeria).

source.title: Our World in Data — GHG Emissions per kg of Food (Poore & Nemecek 2018)
source.note: Peanuts — emissions_per_kg: 3.2 kg CO2e/kg (P&N 2018 groundnuts/peanuts; P&N reports ~3.2 kg CO2e/kg for groundnuts specifically, higher than generic tree nuts; tillage, drying, and crop processing contribute above the N-fixation offset).

source.title: Mekonnen & Hoekstra — Water Footprint of Crops (Report 47)
source.note: Peanuts — water_per_kg: 3,740 L/kg; averaged with secondary sources → final 3,537 L/kg. Report 47 gives 2,782 L/kg for in-shell peanuts; 3,740 reflects shelled/edible kernel basis after accounting for ~74% kernel recovery. Difference is within expected range for basis discrepancy.
